intro: Leptorumohra sinomiqueliana, also known as the Japanese ophioglossum, is a species of fern belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and immune system disorders, due to its potential to lower oxidative stress and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptorumohra sinomiqueliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to support overall health and well-being.
